CorrectnessSpec.description: Layer 1: Hard pass/fail. Any failure blocks the CI pipeline.

expected_in_answer.description: Strings that must ALL appear in the answer (case-insensitive, AND logic)
expected_in_answer.title: Expected In Answer

any_expected_in_answer.description: At least ONE of these strings must appear in the answer (case-insensitive, OR logic)
any_expected_in_answer.title: Any Expected In Answer

not_in_answer.description: Strings that must NOT appear in the answer
not_in_answer.title: Not In Answer

exact_match.description: Answer must equal this string exactly (after stripping whitespace)
exact_match.title: Exact Match

regex_match.description: Answer must match this regex pattern
regex_match.title: Regex Match

json_schema.description: Answer must parse as JSON conforming to this schema
json_schema.title: Json Schema

llm_judge.description: LLM-as-a-judge rubrics; evaluated after deterministic checks
llm_judge.title: Llm Judge

safety_check.description: Safety evaluation rubric (treated as correctness-tier hard fail)

refutes_premise.description: When True, the agent is expected to correct a false premise in the query. Skips expected_in_answer/not_in_answer checks (they don't apply to refutals) and injects a built-in 'false premise correction' rubric into llm_judge.
refutes_premise.title: Refutes Premise

CorrectnessSpec.title: CorrectnessSpec

CostSpec.description: Layer 3: Efficiency budget. Exceedances produce warnings, not failures.

max_cost_multiplier.description: Max allowed cost as a multiple of the golden baseline (e.g. 2.0 = 2×)
max_cost_multiplier.title: Max Cost Multiplier

max_total_tokens.description: Maximum total tokens (input + output) across all LLM calls
max_total_tokens.title: Max Total Tokens

max_llm_calls.description: Maximum number of LLM API calls
max_llm_calls.title: Max Llm Calls

CostSpec.title: CostSpec

GoldenQuery.description: A single test case in the AgentCI spec.

query.description: The input stimulus sent to the agent
query.title: Query

description.description: Human-readable description of what this test validates
description.title: Description

span_assertions.description: Span-level assertions evaluated against trace data. Hard-fails on any assertion failure (like Correctness layer). Use to assert on data flowing between nodes/tools.
span_assertions.title: Span Assertions

GoldenQuery.title: GoldenQuery

scale.description: Score anchors, e.g. ['1: Off-topic', '2: Partially relevant', '3: Fully correct']
scale.title: Scale

context_file.description: Path to a reference document used as ground truth for this rubric. The judge is instructed to evaluate the answer ONLY against this file's contents, ignoring prior training knowledge. Path is resolved relative to the spec file location.
context_file.title: Context File

MatchMode.description: How to compare the agent's tool sequence against the golden baseline.
MatchMode.title: MatchMode

PathSpec.description: Layer 2: Trajectory evaluation. Exceedances produce warnings, not failures.

max_tool_calls.description: Maximum number of tool calls allowed
max_tool_calls.title: Max Tool Calls

expected_tools.description: Tools that should be called. A missing expected tool produces a WARN (recall gates at 1.0 unless min_tool_recall loosens it). An explicit empty list asserts that NO tools are called.
expected_tools.title: Expected Tools

forbidden_tools.description: Tools that must NOT be called (safety boundary — hard fail if violated)
forbidden_tools.title: Forbidden Tools

max_loops.description: Maximum consecutive repeated tool invocations (loop detection). Default 3.
max_loops.title: Max Loops

match_mode.description: How to compare tool sequences against the golden baseline

min_tool_recall.description: |expected ∩ used| / |expected|. Defaults to 1.0 when expected_tools is set.
min_tool_recall.title: Min Tool Recall

expected_tool_sequence.description: Expected tool call sequence (ordered list). Mismatch produces WARN, not hard fail. Example: ['retrieve_docs', 'rerank', 'generate_answer']
expected_tool_sequence.title: Expected Tool Sequence

expected_handoffs_available.description: All agents that should be reachable as handoff targets
expected_handoffs_available.title: Expected Handoffs Available

PathSpec.title: PathSpec

RetrievalSpec.description:
Layer 2.5: Retrieval assertions — deterministic checks on the retriever
tool's captured result.

Result-interpretation contract (eng review 2026-07-05, binding): the layer
reads `ToolCall.result` with explicit rules and SKIPs whenever it cannot —
unparseable results, uncaptured results — never guesses. Empty means None,
[], "", whitespace-only, or a literal match on `empty_markers`; anything
else is non-empty. Retrieval never hard-fails in v1: exceedances WARN.

tool.description: Name of the retriever tool whose captured result is evaluated
tool.title: Tool

forbid_empty.description: WARN when the retriever returned nothing but the agent still produced a substantive answer (an ungrounded answer). An answer matching `refusal_markers` counts as a refusal and passes.
forbid_empty.title: Forbid Empty

min_results.description: Minimum number of retrieved items. Counted only when the result parses as a list — otherwise the check SKIPs, never guesses.
min_results.title: Min Results

expected_sources.description: Doc IDs/paths that must appear in the retrieved results (case-insensitive substring match on the serialized result)
expected_sources.title: Expected Sources

facts_in_context.description: Cross-check that every correctness fact term (expected_in_answer / any_expected_in_answer) also appears in the retrieved chunks. Informational-only in v1: reported, never WARNs.
facts_in_context.title: Facts In Context

result_format.description: Optional parsing hint for ToolCall.result: 'list', 'json', or 'text'. Results that don't parse as the hinted format SKIP.
result_format.title: Result Format

empty_markers.description: Literal no-result strings that count as an empty retrieval, e.g. 'No results found' (case-insensitive exact match after strip)
empty_markers.title: Empty Markers

refusal_markers.description: Answer substrings that count as a refusal for forbid_empty (case-insensitive). Overrides the built-in refusal marker list.
refusal_markers.title: Refusal Markers

RetrievalSpec.title: RetrievalSpec

ScenarioSpec.description:
A multi-turn conversation scenario for `ciagent simulate`.

Scripted mode (`turns:` given) is deterministic and needs no persona LLM —
it is the CI / zero-key path. Generative personas (persona/goal without
turns) are the finder path and ship in a later 0.9 phase.

goal.description: What the simulated user is trying to accomplish
goal.title: Goal

turns.description: Scripted user messages (deterministic mode); the conversation ends when these are exhausted or max_turns is reached
turns.title: Turns

per_turn.description: Checks evaluated on EVERY turn's trace

outcome.description: Checks evaluated once at the END of the conversation as the verdict — never as a stop condition

ScenarioSpec.title: ScenarioSpec

SpanAssert.description: A single assertion on a field within a matched span.

type.description: Assertion type: contains, not_contains, regex, equals, or llm_judge

field.description: Dotted path to the span field to extract, e.g. 'attributes.tool.args.query' or 'output_data'
field.title: Field

rule.description: Natural language rule for llm_judge assertions
rule.title: Rule

value.description: Expected value for contains, not_contains, regex, or equals assertions
value.title: Value

threshold.description: Minimum passing score for llm_judge assertions (normalised 0–1)
threshold.title: Threshold

SpanAssert.title: SpanAssert

SpanAssertType.description: The type of assertion to perform on a span field.
SpanAssertType.title: SpanAssertType

SpanAssertionSpec.description: Span-level assertion: select one or more spans and assert on their fields.

selector.description: Selects which span(s) to assert against

asserts.description: One or more assertions to run against each matched span
asserts.title: Asserts

SpanAssertionSpec.title: SpanAssertionSpec

SpanKindSelector.description: Which type of span to target in span assertions.
SpanKindSelector.title: SpanKindSelector

SpanSelector.description: Selects a span from the trace by kind and name.

kind.description: Type of span to target (TOOL, NODE, HANDOFF, GUARDRAIL, LLM)

name.description: Name of the tool, node, or agent to match
name.title: Name

SpanSelector.title: SpanSelector

StopWhen.description:
Explicit early-exit condition for a scenario.

Termination is deterministic and event-based only (eng review, binding):
never judge-based, never keyword-triggered. A scenario stops early when the
named concrete event is observed in a turn's trace.

tool_called.description: Stop after a turn in which the agent called this tool
tool_called.title: Tool Called

StopWhen.title: StopWhen

TurnChecks.description: Layer checks applied inside a scenario (per-turn or as the outcome).
TurnChecks.title: TurnChecks

baseline_dir.description: Path to the directory where versioned baseline JSON files are stored.
baseline_dir.title: Baseline Dir

runner.description: Python dotted path to the agent runner callable, e.g. 'myagent.run:run_agent'. The function must accept (query: str) and return an ciagent.models.Trace. When set, 'ciagent test' can invoke the agent directly without pytest.
runner.title: Runner

conversation_runner.description: Python dotted path to the multi-turn runner callable for `ciagent simulate`, e.g. 'myagent.run:respond'. The function must accept (messages: list[dict]) with {'role', 'content'} entries and return the assistant's reply as a str (or a ciagent.models.Trace). Fresh state per scenario; history is passed explicitly.
conversation_runner.title: Conversation Runner

defaults.description: Default correctness/path/cost settings applied to all queries unless overridden
defaults.title: Defaults

judge_config.description: Global LLM judge settings: model, temperature, ensemble, structured_output
judge_config.title: Judge Config

persona_config.description: Persona LLM settings for generative simulate scenarios: model (defaults to a cheap haiku-class model) and temperature (default 0.7)
persona_config.title: Persona Config
